methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Blitzpanel is a pre-seed stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Caleb Ho. The relevant comp universe consists of 9 entities across the sector: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The robotics and manufacturing sector yielded 7 same-sector comparables, but none satisfied the filtering criteria for stage-equivalence or valuation-tier proximity. Applying the full-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity in maturity and capital intensity across this universe. Without a minimum of three pre-seed or adjacent-stage robotics companies with disclosed metrics, this triangulation vector does not provide reliable anchoring.

Given the absence of filtered peer multiples and disclosed metrics on Blitzpanel, a valuation range cannot be responsibly derived from the available data. The target company's early stage and the fragmentation of its peer set mean that conventional comparable-company analysis is premature. Future valuation work should track disclosed funding rounds and metrics from the named private peers (particularly Bedrock Robotics and Dexterity, which operate in similar subsectors) as these companies mature and transparency increases. At this stage, valuation would more appropriately rest on first-principles analysis of capital requirements, market size, and founder track record rather than multiples-based methods.
